jobs.source_id: ohmconnect-Senior Technical Implementation Manager, VPP (Remote, US)
jobs.title: Senior Technical Implementation Manager, VPP (Remote, US)
jobs.description: Who We Are Renew Home is on a mission to change how we power the world by making it easier for customers to save energy and money at home as part of the largest residential virtual power plant in North America. We partner with industry-leading brands to better manage residential energy for users by prioritizing efficiency, savings, and comfort — and cleaner energy for everyone. We are an Equal Opportunity employer striving to create a diverse, equitable, and inclusive work environment where everyone feels that they have a voice that is heard. We strongly encourage candidates to check out our website at www.renewhome.com to learn more about the world-changing work we are doing. Role Summary Renew Home's VPP delivery portfolio is expanding — more programs, new markets, and new utility partnerships, all moving in parallel. If you are a strategic operator and exceptional executor who thrives creating structure in ambiguity, making complex systems clear and functional, and is as comfortable running a high-stakes utility partner meeting as managing a detailed program plan — this role was built for you. The Senior Technical Implementation Manager, VPP owns the end-to-end process of bringing first-of-kind VPP programs from concept to live operation. You'll sit at the center of our VPP Delivery portfolio — spanning utility programs, market VPP operations, and new program types in the pipeline — and serve as the connective tissue between teams during the most critical phase of any program: launch. You'll own programs from initial scoping through steady-state handoff, building the operational infrastructure, coordinating across teams, and making each launch more repeatable than the last. You'll work alongside a team of analysts who own steady-state execution and tracking — together supporting the full program delivery lifecycle. You actively look for ways to streamline, reduce friction, and automate across the implementation process -- for internal teams and external partners alike. Think of this role as part Program Architect, part Launch Manager — you design the operational model, coordinate the launch, and ensure it is set up for success in steady state. What You Will Do Program Scoping & Design Lead the intake and scoping process for new VPP programs — from initial concept through operational design and implementation plan. Proactively communicate program risks upward — translating technical and operational blockers into clear risk narratives for leadership, with recommended mitigations and decision asks. Define success metrics, tracking frameworks, and performance baselines before launch, so every program is instrumented from day one. Partner with commercial and technical teams to translate financial assumptions into operational requirements. Document program architecture — SOPs, runbooks, process flows — in shared knowledge systems so client-facing and technical teams can operate independently. Cross-Functional Implementation Coordination Drive implementation and delivery readiness across commercial and technical teams — owning the critical path, tracking dependencies, and surfacing risks early. Develop and maintain scalable processes for standard program implementations while owning bespoke scoping for non-standard or first-of-kind programs — building the framework that turns edge cases into repeatable playbooks. Manage programs across all phases from intake and scoping through testing, go-live, and steady-state handoff. Serve as the primary internal point of contact for commercial teams during program build and launch; coordinate with Client Success, who owns the external client relationship. Facilitate formal launch reviews and ensure all cross-functional sign-offs are completed before a program goes live; synthesize inputs into exec-ready summaries for leadership go/no-go decisions. Assess and escalate program risks based on development activities across teams. Handoff & Steady-State Setup Define clear handoff criteria

jobs.title: Business Controller
jobs.description: Keywords Studios is an international technical and creative services provider to the global video games industry. Established in 1998 and headquartered in Dublin, Keywords now has over 70 studios located across Europe, North America, South America and Asia. We provide integrated art production, engineering, audio services, testing, localization and player support services across 50 languages and 14 game platforms to a blue-chip client base in more than 20 countries. With over 12,000 passionate gaming employees around the world, we provide services to video game developers and publishers alike. Financial Reporting & Analysis: Prepare monthly reporting for multiple cost centres and review consolidated group figures to ensure alignment with forecast/budget expectations. Variance Management: Proactively liaise with global teams to identify, analyse, and document reasons for budget and forecast variances. Forecasting Support: Assist in the monthly refinement of financial forecasts to reflect real-time business changes. jobs.description: About Crumdale Specialty : Crumdale Specialty is a diversified insurance firm providing custom, self-funded healthcare solutions to a limited distribution network of brokers, consultants, and agents nationwide. Ranked on the Inc. 5000 Fastest Growing Companies and Best Workplaces, we leverage industry expertise, superior talent, data analytics, and a disruptive mindset to manufacture, underwrite, and administer agile, transparent, and cost-saving solutions. We optimize the fragmented health benefits supply chain to reduce health benefit costs and create better outcomes for employers and employees. At Crumdale, people come first. We strive to make a positive impact on the people we serve. We believe this starts with the passion and purpose of our team. Our company culture is rooted in alignment, innovation, and integrity. About the Job: Crumdale Specialty is seeking a Captive Sales Advisor to partner with Regional Sales Directors to win alternative funding opportunities specifically by turning “captive” from a conceptual idea into a clear, finance‑ready decision for employers. This role elevates the effectiveness of our field teams by simplifying technical components of self-funding, captives, stop‑loss strategy, and cost-containment solutions. The Captive SME plays a critical part in shaping deal strategy, enabling producers, and ensuring that opportunities move efficiently from discovery to close. Key Responsibilities: Serve as the subject matter expert on complex captive and alternative funding cases. Equip producers with concise talk tracks, discovery paths, and executive-friendly explanations of risk, funding mechanics, governance, and key tradeoffs. Support regional sales leaders in positioning Crumdale’s captive solutions competitively and effectively. Build, activate, and maintain referral networks across brokers and consultants. Make it simple for external partners to introduce captive strategies. Deliver training, tools, and confidence-building resources that expand captive pipeline opportunities. Coordinate with underwriting, operations, account management, and solution partners to ensure cohesive deal execution. Translate discovery findings into credible proposals and ensure readiness for financial and operational evaluation. Maintain disciplined timelines and ensure seamless handoffs into onboarding. Bring structure to the alternative funding sales motion with clear KPIs, next steps, and progress tracking. Help internal teams prioritize support based on probability to close. Ensure opportunities do not stall in “maybe” by driving accountability and clarity throughout the process. Develop field-ready materials that increase speed-to-proposal and close rates. Contribute to internal training, content creation, and tools that simplify complexity for both buyers and brokers. Represent Crumdale’s captive capabilities with clarity and authority in the market. Requirements Deep experience in self-funding, group captives, stop‑loss strategy, and employer funding models (mid‑market focus). Background working within national brokerage platforms or high‑growth consulting environments. Strong executive communication skills with ability to translate complex concepts into simple, defensible employer decisions. Proven ability to partner with frontline producers and guide complex deals through a disciplined sales process. Ability to orchestrate cross-functional teams from underwriting through operations to deliver accurate, timely proposals. High commercial acumen and strong relationship‑building skills in both direct sales and channel environments. Benefits At Crumdale, we strive to provide a comprehensive and supportive benefits package to ensure the well-being of our employees. jobs.title: Senior Social Media Strategist
jobs.description: Please read before applying : We review every application carefully, and all fields (including the required 60–90 second video) must be fully completed and relevant; applications that are incomplete or use AI-generated responses will not be considered. If you’re unable to meet these requirements, we kindly ask that you refrain from applying. Full-time • Remote At Boldin , we believe financial confidence should be accessible to everyone. Money decisions shape our lives, yet too often people are left without the clarity or tools they need to make informed choices. We exist to change that. Boldin is a comprehensive financial planning platform that helps people understand their financial picture, make smarter decisions with their money and time, and plan for the future with confidence. With over $20M raised and strong momentum, Boldin is entering a pivotal phase of growth. This is an opportunity to join a mission-driven team and help shape the future. About the Role Boldin is not just a financial planning platform — it’s a movement. Social is where that movement becomes visible, shareable, and contagious. The Senior Social Media Strategist is responsible for turning Boldin into a daily, high-value presence in people’s lives — creating content that sparks conversation, builds trust, and drives meaningful engagement across platforms. This role sits at the intersection of storytelling, culture, data, and distribution. You will own and grow Boldin ’s presence across YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, X, and LinkedIn — with a mandate to double followers, create viral moments, and materially increase engagement and reach. You will collaborate closely with Content, Video, PR, Brand, and Paid Marketing to ensure Boldin shows up consistently, intelligently, and memorably across every channel. Key Responsibilities: Own and grow Boldin ’s social presence Manage and execute posting across YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, X, and LinkedIn Develop and maintain a cross-platform content calendar aligned with brand, product, and growth priorities Ensure consistency in voice, tone, and messaging across all channels Create high-performing, shareable content Write and produce posts that educate, inspire, and spark conversation Translate financial concepts into clear, compelling, culturally relevant content Identify and execute on trends, formats, and narratives that drive virality Partner with video and design to create engaging short-form and long-form content Drive engagement and community Actively manage comments, conversations, and community interactions Build a sense of participation, not just broadcasting Develop repeatable formats that encourage sharing, saving, and discussion Optimize for growth and performance Track and improve key metrics: follower growth, engagement rates, reach, shares, saves Test and iterate on formats, hooks, timing, and messaging Identify platform-specific strategies to maximize performance Collaborate across teams Work with Align with Paid Marketing on amplification and testing Support company leadership, amplifying thought leadership Success reporting Deliver weekly, monthly, and quarterly reports on traffic and funnel performance for social channels KPIS + Targets At least 2-3x growth in followers across all major platforms by year end 3x++ increases in engagement rate (likes, comments, shares, saves) Increased social-driven traffic and brand awareness Creation of repeatable, high-performing content formats Measurable contribution to overall brand and earned traffic growth Qualifications: You are a storyteller, strategist, and operator — someone who understands how ideas spread and how to turn content into momentum. jobs.title: Lead Web Engineer
jobs.description: 🌱 Genki means "healthy, energetic, and full of spirit". That's us. 🚀 We're a fully remote, profitable startup of about 25 people, five years in and growing steadily. 🌍 We build tailored health insurance for long-term travelers like digital nomads, serving over 75,000 people in 200 countries. 💚 Our vision is to help them stay healthy wherever they go, far beyond insurance. We're hiring a Lead Web Engineer to own the technical direction of all our internal and public web projects : unify what's grown organically, and build the foundation for what comes next. You'll start as the sole web engineer, with the team growing around you over time. You'll work alongside two existing non-web developers, report directly to the CEO, and collaborate closely with our designer to shape what we build and how it looks. We need someone who builds carefully and ships fast, and sees no contradiction between the two. Someone who has seen enough production systems to know what matters (security, reliability, maintainability) and who uses AI agents to build better software, faster. Part of the job is also building a setup where team members from all departments can build software themselves, with standardized review, security, and deployment so that shipping stays safe as more people contribute. Show us what you've got. Send us a link to your GitHub profile, code samples, prompts, a vibe-coded job application, or whatever expresses your experience best! Requirements Experience You've built and shipped production web applications end-to-end, from static sites to complex SPAs, and you know where the real problems hide in each. You've made major architectural bets and stayed long enough to learn whether they were right. Proven experience with cross-project infrastructure: shared component libraries, CI/CD pipelines, monorepo setups, deployment automation. Strong understanding of web security, performance optimization, and browser capabilities. You have a sharp eye for detail, from pixel-perfect implementation to clean, consistent code. You use AI-agentic development tools daily (Claude Code, Codex, or similar) and know how to steer them effectively, including when to trust the output. You've set up and maintained CI/CD, testing strategies, and developer tooling for teams, not just yourself. Our current tech stack Languages & frameworks: TypeScript, React, React Router, Next.js (being phased out), SCSS Modules, Astro Bundling: Vite Design: Figma AI-agentic development: Claude Code with custom plugin (open to exploring alternatives) Infrastructure: Cloudflare, Railway APIs: GraphQL Working style You care deeply about user experience. You pay close attention to the details and take pride in getting them right. You think in systems, not screens. You care about how projects share infrastructure, how deployments stay safe, and how other developers will understand what you built. You're excited about enabling non-engineers to ship software and know how to make that safe through good tooling and guardrails. You're comfortable making decisions when not all the information is there yet. You thrive in a fully remote, async-first team and know how to communicate clearly without meetings for everything. You're as open to different ideas, opinions, and to people with different cultures, backgrounds, and interests as we are. Benefits 💰 Up to 10,000 EUR/month (depending on experience) with employee equity participation through a vesting program 🏝 Fully remote, from anywhere in the world & live as a digital nomad if you want to (the country list above are just examples) 🕗 Flexible hours, startup atmosphere 🌍 Most of the team is in Europe and Southeast Asia, with a few hours of daily overlap around German business hours 💻 Company-issued and managed MacBook, plus a desk in your favorite co-working space ✈️ 25 days paid annual leave 🤝 Regular team off-sites to connect in person Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Mid-Level Backend Developer
jobs.description: 🌱 Genki means "healthy, energetic, and full of spirit". That's us. 🚀 We're a fully remote, profitable startup of about 25 people, five years in and growing steadily. 🌍 We build tailored health insurance for long-term travelers like digital nomads, serving over 75,000 people in 200 countries. 💚 Our vision is to help them stay healthy wherever they go, far beyond insurance. We're looking for a Mid-Level Backend Developer to help build and evolve the core system that powers our products and operations. You'll join as the second developer in our backend team, reporting to our Lead Backend Developer. Our backend runs on Kotlin with a custom framework built around event sourcing. It's not yet fully documented, so you'll need to be comfortable reading code, asking questions, and finding your way through a system that's still being shaped. You'll build new features, improve testability and reliability, and help evolve the architecture over time. The product is complex and spans multiple domains. You'll need to build deep understanding quickly and be comfortable making decisions with incomplete information. We're moving fast, increasingly with AI-agentic development tools. You should have some hands-on experience with these tools and a genuine drive to go deeper. Show us what you've got. Send us a link to your GitHub profile, code samples, prompts, a vibe-coded job application, or whatever expresses your experience best! Requirements Experience You've worked on larger backend systems in production and understand the complexity they bring. You've worked in complex product domains and can ramp up quickly on business logic that isn't always obvious. You're comfortable with event sourcing, event-driven architecture, or similar patterns. You care about code quality: readable code, meaningful tests, and clean APIs. You have some experience with AI-agentic development tools (Claude Code, Codex, or similar) and want to go deeper. Our current tech stack Language: Kotlin with coroutines Framework: Custom, built around event sourcing Billing: Stripe Infrastructure: Heroku (migrating to Railway) AI-agentic development: Claude Code with custom plugin APIs: GraphQL Working style You pay close attention to the details and take pride in getting them right. You're curious and self-driven. You dig into unfamiliar code, ask good questions, and figure things out. You'd rather understand why something works than just make it work. You're comfortable making decisions when not all the information is there yet. You've worked in a startup or scaleup and know what that pace and ambiguity feels like. You thrive in a fully remote, async-first team and know how to communicate clearly without meetings for everything. You're as open to different ideas, opinions, and to people with different cultures, backgrounds, and interests as we are. Benefits 💰 Up to 5,000 EUR/month (depending on experience) with employee equity participation through a vesting program 🏝 Fully remote, from anywhere in the world & live as a digital nomad if you want to (the country list above are just examples) 🕗 Flexible hours, startup atmosphere 🌍 Most of the team is in Europe and Southeast Asia, with a few hours of daily overlap around German business hours 💻 Company-issued and managed MacBook, plus a desk in your favorite co-working space ✈️ 25 days paid annual leave 🤝 Regular team off-sites to connect in person Originally posted on Himalayas

Ongoing retainer not a one-off project About the Role We’re looking for a hands-on, growth-oriented Junior SEO Specialist to join our in-house Organic Marketing team. This is a delivery role across the full SEO stack: technical, on-page, content, keyword research, link building, and AI search readiness. implementation (Product, Review, Organization, Breadcrumb, FAQ) • Hreflang corrections, international SEO debugging, ccTLD and subdomain consistency • Sitemap, canonical, robots.txt, and indexation page indexation • Redirect mapping and post-launch QA for migrations and category changes On-page and content SEO • Implement on-page SEO across 30+ country sites: title tags, meta descriptions, headings, internal linking • Update and optimise existing content: refresh underperforming pages, improve depth, fix on-page gaps • Keyword research and mapping: build and maintain keyword portfolios, competitor gap analysis • Content briefing support for the content team • Localisation QA on SEO elements across translations AI search readiness • Support our AI search visibility: structured data and schema that helps LLMs and AI search parse our pages • Monitor visibility in SGE, AI Overviews, and LLM-driven search, and flag where we’re cited or missing • Help adapt on-page content and structured data so our brand surfaces in AI-generated answers Link building and monitoring • Run consistent outreach campaigns to earn quality backlinks and brand mentions • Daily Google Search jobs.title: Office Administrator and Accounting Specialist
jobs.description: Job Summary: The Office Administrator and Accounting Specialist is responsible for general office duties including supporting the financial operations of the organization by performing a range of accounting duties related to accounts receivable, accounts payable, and general ledger functions. This role ensures accurate financial records, timely processing of transactions, and adherence to internal controls and compliance standards. Core Duties Depending on business needs, responsibilities may include: Accounts Payable Functions: Process vendor invoices, ensuring accuracy, proper approvals, and timely payments. Match purchase orders and receipts to invoices and investigate discrepancies. Prepare and process payments via check, ACH, or wire transfer. Maintain vendor records and respond to vendor inquiries. Assist with month-end closing and accruals related to AP. Accounts Receivable Functions: Generate and distribute customer invoices in a timely and accurate manner. Apply customer payments to the appropriate accounts and follow up on outstanding receivables. Monitor aging reports and perform collection activities as needed. Investigate and resolve billing discrepancies or customer concerns. Assist with revenue recognition and related reporting. General Duties: Reconcile account balances and support general ledger entries. Maintain accurate and organized financial documentation. Collaborate with internal teams (e.g., procurement, sales, operations) to resolve accounting issues. Support internal and external audits by providing necessary documentation. Contribute to process improvements and efficiency initiatives. Office Administration Support Other duties as assigned. Job Requirements Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (or equivalent experience of 3 years). At least 2 years of experience in accounts payable, accounts receivable, or general accounting. Familiarity with accounting software (e.g., Workday, SAP, Oracle, QuickBooks, NetSuite). Strong understanding of basic accounting principles and internal controls. Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other MS Office applications. Excellent attention to detail, organizational, and time management skills. Strong communication and interpersonal skills. Preferred Qualifications: Experience working in a shared services or high-volume transaction environment. Exposure to ERP systems (especially NetSuite). Knowledge of GAAP. XPEL is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Digital Marketing Trainee (English Native) - Greece
jobs.description: Largest designer glasses e-commerce retailer worldwide (smartbuyglasses.com) Multicultural team representing over 20 nationalities Fast growing, dynamic and young company culture Company Profile SmartBuyGlasses Optical Group, operated by Motion Global Ltd., is the world’s leading designer eyewear e-retailer. With operations spanning Asia Pacific, Europe, and the Americas, we are the market leader in over 30 countries. Our portfolio of 350+ designer brands includes Ray-Ban, Tom Ford, Gucci, and many others. Our team consists of over 200 professionals from 20+ nationalities, reflecting a young, dynamic, and multicultural environment. At SmartBuyGlasses , we foster a culture of rapid career growth, entrepreneurship, and self-development, with a focus on empowering employees to take on managerial roles quickly through passion and performance. For more information, visit: www.smartbuyglasses.com . Position Overview: Digital Marketing Trainee Are you ready to fast-track your digital marketing career? Our accelerated Digital Marketing Trainee Program is designed to turn ambitious individuals into versatile marketing professionals. Over six months, you’ll immerse yourself in hands-on training, gaining expertise across key areas like copywriting, content creation, SEO, social media, and more. You’ll learn from industry leaders and contribute directly to real-world projects that drive results. This program is perfect for someone passionate about e-commerce, content creation, and all things digital. If you’re eager to dive deep into fashion and eyewear while building expertise across marketing disciplines, this role is for you! Key Responsibilities: Research and craft high-impact content for websites, press releases, social media, and other marketing channels. Work alongside talented designers and photographers to develop integrated, eye-catching content. Gain in-depth knowledge of top eyewear brands and industry trends, staying on top of new releases. Ensure brand consistency and strong digital presence across all communication channels. Collaborate on cutting-edge digital marketing strategies and execution plans. Qualifications: Native-level English fluency with flawless grammar and writing skills. A passion for writing, blogging, and digital marketing. Basic Photoshop skills (a plus). Strong interest in design trends, marketing, technology, and social media. Skills & Requirements: Strong research and analytical abilities. Creative and concise writing style. Ability to work independently and demonstrate a proactive, “can-do” attitude. Excellent time management skills and the ability to meet tight deadlines. A growth mindset with the desire to rapidly develop and take on new challenges. Trainee Program Details: 6-month traineeship with the potential for a full-time role based on performance. Comprehensive training across all areas of online marketing from industry experts. Fast-track career development for top performers. Location: Remote Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Freelance Mathematics Tutor - Primary (Grade 1 - 3)
jobs.description: Summary We are looking to hire a Tutor to join us in our mission to educate the world. The successful candidate shall be responsible for conducting online sessions on “Mathematics” and engaging the students during the sessions and helping them prepare for the exams. Responsibilities Conduct online sessions for students through Nagwa Classes according to MoE curriculum to support the learning process Run engagement activities to ensure efficient learning outcomes and communicate with students before and after the sessions through Nagwa Classes Follow up on the student’s progress through the assignments sent after each session through Nagwa Classes to ensure that the learning outcomes are reached Provide students with constructive feedback to help students develop their skills and subject knowledge to improve learning outcomes Qualifications & Work Experience Bachelor’s degree in any relevant discipline Ability to collect, interpret, and summarize data 2-4 years of relevant teaching experience Experience in differentiated instruction to deliver different learning needs and styles Previous online tutoring is a plus Proficient in Arabic Intermediate in English (or language of the subject) Having a laptop or desktop computer is a must Behavioural Competencies Strong communication skills in verbal and written Leadership skills Time management Accountability and ownership Problem solving Attention to detail Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Security Backend Engineer
jobs.description: Description Company is the pioneer of Active ASPM, securing the modern software supply chain. We cut through alert noise to surface the critical 5% of risks that are truly reachable and exploitable. We're hiring a Backend Engineer for our Security Research group to build the systems thatpower our open-source intelligence work - ingesting public package ecosystems (NPM, PyPI),monitoring them continuously, and detecting malicious behavior at scale.This is a highly autonomous IC role where you’ll own projects end-to-end - transforming researcher prototypes into scalable production systems. Responsibilities: Build scalable scraping and ingestion pipelines for public package registries (NPM, PyPI, etc.) Design and maintain distributed systems based on APIs, workers, queues, and databases Develop detection mechanisms for: malicious install hooks, embedded binaries, obfuscation techniques, suspicious package behavior Build and improve risk-scoring algorithms to prioritize real threats Work closely with security researchers to productionize detection capabilities Requirements Requirements: 5+ years of backend development experience with Python and/or Node.js / TypeScript Hands-on experience with large-scale scraping systems Strong knowledge of distributed architectures: queues, workers, PostgreSQL, Redis Production experience with Docker / docker-compose Strong ownership mindset and ability to work autonomously Full professional English proficiency ﻿Strong Advantage: Malware analysis or reverse engineering experience Familiarity with ELF / PE / Mach-O formats Background in security research or software supply-chain security Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Videographer – Lion Health Lab (Part-Time, Remote)
jobs.description: APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS: CURRENT PENN STATE EMPLOYEE (faculty, staff, technical service, or student), please login to Workday to complete the internal application process . Please do not apply here, apply internally through Workday. CURRENT PENN STATE STUDENT (not employed previously at the university) and seeking employment with Penn State, please login to Workday to complete the student application process. Please do not apply here, apply internally through Workday. If you are NOT a current employee or student, please click “Apply” and complete the application process for external applicants . Approval of remote and hybrid work is not guaranteed regardless of work location. For additional information on remote work at Penn State, see Notice to Out of State Applicants . JOB DESCRIPTION AND POSITION REQUIREMENTS Videographer – Lion Health Lab (Part-Time, Remote) JOB DESCRIPTION AND POSITION REQUIREMENTS The Lion Health Lab, directed by Dr. Janette Watkins in the Department of Kinesiology at Penn State University , seeks a creative and self-motivated Videographer to support digital media production for research communication, recruitment, and outreach. The Lion Health Lab’s mission is to “Protect the Pride—building brain and heart health one generation at a time.” The lab conducts community-based, family-focused health promotion and physical activity interventions, translating science into accessible and engaging public communication. This position is fully remote and ideal for a skilled professional interested in visual storytelling, video editing, and social media content creation that highlights research impact and community partnerships. RESPONSIBILITIES Record, edit, and produce short- and long-form video content highlighting lab projects, community programs, and research findings. Create promotional materials for the lab’s website, YouTube, and social media platforms (e.g., Instagram Reels, highlight videos). Collaborate with the Principal Investigator and lab team to plan, storyboard, and ensure brand alignment with Penn State and Lion Health Lab standards. Edit and organize media files, ensuring accessibility and consistency across communication channels. Maintain a professional, creative approach to storytelling that aligns with the university’s mission and standards for research communication. EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE Required Qualifications: Demonstrated experience in video production, editing, and post-production. Proficiency with video editing software such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or equivalent. Strong visual storytelling and creative direction skills. Excellent organizational and communication skills with the ability to work independently in a remote environment. Reliable access to video-editing software, high-speed internet, and appropriate computer hardware. Preferred Qualifications: Experience producing social media content (Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, etc.). Familiarity with topics related to health, fitness, or community-based programs. Skills in photography, motion graphics, or digital design. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION Employment Type: Part-time, hourly (approximately 10–15 hours per week). Work Location: Fully remote within the United States. Pay Rate: $30/hour. Schedule: Flexible; occasional virtual meetings with the research team.
